    35→# Internal: Log LLM invocation completion with timing and cost metrics
    36→# Args:
    37→#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, openai-api, custom)
    38→#   $2 - model name
    39→#   $3 - wall time in milliseconds
    40→# Reads globals set by provider implementations:
    41→#   _LLM_DURATION_MS - API-reported duration (may differ from wall time)
    42→#   _LLM_COST_USD - Cost in USD or "unknown"
    43→#   _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens or "unknown"
    44→#   _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens or "unknown"
    45→_llm_log_completion() {
    46→    local provider="$1"
    47→    local model="$2"
    48→    local wall_time_ms="$3"
    49→
    50→    # Build timing component (show api_time only if different from wall_time)
    51→    local timing="wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms"
    52→    if [ -n "${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-}" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "unknown" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "$wall_time_ms" ]; then
    53→        timing="${timing}, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS}ms"
    54→    fi
    55→
    56→    # Build token component
    57→    local tokens="unknown"
    58→    if [ "${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ] || [ "${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ]; then
    59→        tokens="${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"
    60→    fi
    61→
    62→    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=$provider, model=$model, $timing, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=$tokens"
    63→}
    64→
    65→# Find LLM binary for specified provider
    66→# Args:
    67→#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
    68→# Returns:
    69→#   Path to binary
    70→# Exits:
    71→#   3 if binary not found
    72→llm_find_bin() {
    73→    local provider="$1"
    74→    local bin_path=""
    75→    local config_var=""
    76→    local default_name=""
    77→
    78→    case "$provider" in
    79→        claude-cli)
    80→            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
    81→            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
    82→            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")

    88→            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)

    90→                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
    91→                return 0
    92→            fi
    93→
    94→            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    95→            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    96→            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
    97→                echo "$local_claude"
    98→                return 0
    99→            fi
   100→
   101→            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
   102→            local path_claude
   103→            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
   104→            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
   105→                echo "$path_claude"

   113→            ;;
   114→        custom)

   118→        openai-api)

   133→            echo "curl"

   143→            return 0
   144→            ;;
   145→        *)

   148→            ;;
   149→    esac
   150→
   151→    # Check config variable first
   152→    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
   153→        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")

   155→            echo "$bin_path"
   156→            return 0
   157→        fi
   158→    fi

   161→    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
   162→        local path_bin
   163→        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
   164→        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
   165→            echo "$path_bin"

   167→        fi

   170→    # Not found
   171→    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
   172→    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then

   174→    fi

   177→
   178→# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
   179→# Args:
   180→#   $1 - model name
   181→#   $2 - prompt text
   182→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   183→# Returns:
   184→#   Raw output from Claude CLI (text content only)
   185→# Side effects:
   186→#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
   187→#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds
   188→#     _LLM_COST_USD - Total cost in USD (if available)
   189→#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
   190→#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
   191→_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
   192→    local model="$1"
   193→    local prompt="$2"
   194→    local timeout="$3"
   195→
   196→    local claude_bin
   197→    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")
   198→
   199→    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
   200→    local workspace
   201→    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")
   202→
   203→    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
   204→    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"
   205→
   206→    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
   207→    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
   208→    local output
   209→    local claude_errors
   210→    local claude_output_file
   211→    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
   212→    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)
   213→
   214→    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
   215→    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
   216→    local start_ms
   217→    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   218→
   219→    set +e
   220→    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
   221→        --model "$model" \
   222→        --output-format json \
   223→        --setting-sources project \
   224→        <<EOF
   225→$prompt
   226→EOF
   227→    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
   228→    local exit_code=$?
   229→    set -e
   230→
   231→    local end_ms
   232→    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   233→    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))
   234→
   235→    # Read output from file
   236→    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
   237→    rm -f "$claude_output_file"
   238→
   239→    # Check if Claude invocation failed
   240→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   241→        local errors
   242→        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   243→        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   244→        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
   245→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   246→        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
   247→        rm -f "$claude_errors"
   248→        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   249→        return 1
   250→    fi
   251→
   252→    # Check for stderr warnings
   253→    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
   254→        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
   255→    fi
   256→    rm -f "$claude_errors"
   257→
   258→    # Cleanup workspace
   259→    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   260→
   261→    # Extract metadata from JSON response
   262→    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
   263→    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
   264→    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
   265→    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')
   266→
   267→    # Log timing and cost information
   268→    _llm_log_completion "claude-cli" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"
   269→
   270→    # Extract just the result text from JSON
   271→    local result
   272→    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')
   273→
   274→    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
   275→        log_error "Failed to extract result from Claude CLI JSON response"
   276→        log_error "Response: $output"
   277→        return 1
   278→    fi
   279→
   280→    echo "$result"
   281→}

   283→# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
   284→# Args:
   285→#   $1 - model name
   286→#   $2 - prompt text
   287→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   288→# Returns:
   289→#   Text content from OpenAI API
   290→# Side effects:
   291→#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
   292→#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
   293→#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response)
   294→#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
   295→#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
   296→_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
   297→    local model="$1"
   298→    local prompt="$2"
   299→    local timeout="$3"
   300→
   301→    # Get API key from config or environment
   302→    local api_key
   303→    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
   304→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   305→        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
   306→    fi
   307→
   308→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   309→        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
   310→        return 1
   311→    fi
   312→
   313→    # Get endpoint
   314→    local endpoint
   315→    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
   316→    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}
   317→
   318→    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
   319→    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"
   320→
   321→    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
   322→    local escaped_prompt
   323→    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)
   324→
   325→    local payload
   326→    payload=$(cat <<EOF
   327→{
   328→  "model": "$model",
   329→  "messages": [
   330→    {
   331→      "role": "user",
   332→      "content": $escaped_prompt
   333→    }
   334→  ]
   335→}
   336→EOF
   337→)
   338→
   339→    # Invoke API with curl
   340→    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
   341→    local payload_file
   342→    payload_file=$(mktemp)
   343→    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"
   344→
   345→    local output
   346→    local curl_errors
   347→    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
   348→
   349→    local start_ms
   350→    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   351→
   352→    set +e
   353→    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
   354→        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   355→        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
   356→        -d "@$payload_file" \
   357→        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
   358→    local exit_code=$?
   359→    set -e
   360→
   361→    local end_ms
   362→    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   363→    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))
   364→
   365→    # Cleanup payload file
   366→    rm -f "$payload_file"
   367→
   368→    # Check for curl errors
   369→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   370→        local errors
   371→        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   372→        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   373→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   374→        rm -f "$curl_errors"
   375→        return 1
   376→    fi
   377→    rm -f "$curl_errors"
   378→
   379→    # Check for API errors in response
   380→    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   381→        local error_msg
   382→        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
   383→        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
   384→        return 1
   385→    fi
   386→
   387→    # Extract token usage from response
   388→    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
   389→    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response
   390→    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
   391→    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')
   392→
   393→    # Log timing and token information
   394→    _llm_log_completion "openai-api" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"
   395→
   396→    # Extract content from response
   397→    local content
   398→    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')
   399→

   401→        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
   402→        log_error "Response: $output"
   403→        return 1

   406→    echo "$content"

   535→# Internal: Invoke OpenRouter API provider
   536→# Args:
   537→#   $1 - model name
   538→#   $2 - prompt text
   539→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   540→# Returns:
   541→#   Text content from OpenRouter API
   542→# Side effects:
   543→#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
   544→#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
   545→#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
   546→#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
   547→#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
   548→_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
   549→    local model="$1"
   550→    local prompt="$2"
   551→    local timeout="$3"
   552→
   553→    # Get API key from config or environment
   554→    local api_key
   555→    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
   556→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   557→        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
   558→    fi
   559→
   560→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   561→        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
   562→        return 1
   563→    fi
   564→
   565→    # Get endpoint
   566→    local endpoint
   567→    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
   568→    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}
   569→
   570→    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
   571→    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"
   572→
   573→    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
   574→    local escaped_prompt
   575→    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)
   576→
   577→    local payload
   578→    payload=$(cat <<EOF
   579→{
   580→  "model": "$model",
   581→  "messages": [
   582→    {
   583→      "role": "user",
   584→      "content": $escaped_prompt
   585→    }
   586→  ]
   587→}
   588→EOF
   589→)
   590→
   591→    # Invoke API with curl
   592→    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
   593→    local payload_file
   594→    payload_file=$(mktemp)
   595→    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"
   596→
   597→    local output
   598→    local curl_errors
   599→    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
   600→
   601→    local start_ms
   602→    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   603→
   604→    set +e
   605→    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
   606→        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   607→        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
   608→        -d "@$payload_file" \
   609→        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
   610→    local exit_code=$?
   611→    set -e
   612→
   613→    local end_ms
   614→    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   615→    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))
   616→
   617→    # Cleanup payload file
   618→    rm -f "$payload_file"
   619→
   620→    # Check for curl errors
   621→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   622→        local errors
   623→        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   624→        log_error "OpenRouter API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   625→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   626→        rm -f "$curl_errors"
   627→        return 1
   628→    fi
   629→    rm -f "$curl_errors"
   630→
   631→    # Check for API errors in response
   632→    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   633→        local error_msg
   634→        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
   635→        log_error "OpenRouter API returned error: ${error_msg}"
   636→        return 1
   637→    fi
   638→
   639→    # Extract token usage from response
   640→    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
   641→    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response
   642→    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
   643→    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')
   644→
   645→    # Log timing and token information
   646→    _llm_log_completion "openrouter" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"
   647→
   648→    # Extract content from response
   649→    local content
   650→    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')
   651→
   652→    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
   653→        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenRouter response"
   654→        log_error "Response: $output"
   655→        return 1
   656→    fi
   657→
   658→    echo "$content"
   659→}
   660→
   661→# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
   662→# Args:
   663→#   $1 - model name
   664→#   $2 - prompt text
   665→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   666→# Returns:
   667→#   Raw output from custom command
   668→# Side effects:
   669→#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
   670→#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
   671→#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
   672→#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
   673→#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
   674→_llm_invoke_custom() {
   675→    local model="$1"
   676→    local prompt="$2"
   677→    local timeout="$3"
   678→
   679→    local custom_bin
   680→    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")
   681→
   682→    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
   683→        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
   684→        return 1
   685→    fi
   686→
   687→    local command_template
   688→    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")
   689→
   690→    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
   691→        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
   692→        return 1
   693→    fi
   694→
   695→    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
   696→    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
   697→    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"
   698→
   699→    # Write prompt to temporary file
   700→    local prompt_file
   701→    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
   702→    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"
   703→
   704→    # Substitute placeholders in command template
   705→    local command="$command_template"
   706→    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
   707→    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
   708→    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
   709→    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"
   710→
   711→    log_debug "Executing: $command"
   712→
   713→    # Execute command
   714→    local output
   715→    local command_errors
   716→    command_errors=$(mktemp)
   717→
   718→    local start_ms
   719→    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   720→
   721→    set +e
   722→    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
   723→    local exit_code=$?
   724→    set -e
   725→
   726→    local end_ms
   727→    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   728→    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))
   729→
   730→    # Cleanup prompt file
   731→    rm -f "$prompt_file"
   732→
   733→    # Check for errors
   734→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   735→        local errors
   736→        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   737→        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   738→        log_error "Command: $command"
   739→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   740→        rm -f "$command_errors"
   741→        return 1
   742→    fi
   743→
   744→    # Check for stderr warnings
   745→    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
   746→        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
   747→    fi
   748→    rm -f "$command_errors"
   749→
   750→    # Set metadata (unknown for custom provider since format is not standardized)
   751→    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
   752→    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"
   753→    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
   754→    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
   755→
   756→    # Log timing information
   757→    _llm_log_completion "custom" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"
   758→
   759→    echo "$output"
   760→}
   761→
   762→# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
   763→# Args:
   764→#   $1 - model name
   765→#   $2 - prompt text
   766→#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
   767→# Returns:
   768→#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
   769→# Exits:
   770→#   1 on error
   771→llm_invoke() {
   772→    local model="$1"
   773→    local prompt="$2"
   774→    local timeout="${3:-30}"
   775→
   776→    # Get provider from config
   777→    local provider
   778→    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
   779→    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}
   780→
   781→    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"
   782→
   783→    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
   784→    local output
   785→    case "$provider" in
   786→        claude-cli)
   787→            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   788→            ;;
   789→        openai-api)
   790→            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   791→            ;;
   792→        groq)
   793→            output=$(_llm_invoke_groq "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   794→            ;;
   795→        openrouter)
   796→            output=$(_llm_invoke_openrouter "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   797→            ;;
   798→        custom)
   799→            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   800→            ;;
   801→        *)
   802→            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
   803→            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, groq, openrouter, custom"
   804→            return 1
   805→            ;;
   806→    esac
   807→
   808→    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
   809→    local json
   810→    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
   811→
   812→    # Validate JSON
   813→    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
   814→        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
   815→        return 1
   816→    fi
   817→
   818→    echo "$json"
   819→}
   820→
   821→# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
   822→# Args:
   823→#   $1 - LLM output text
   824→# Returns:
   825→#   Extracted JSON
   826→llm_extract_json() {
   827→    local output="$1"
   828→
   829→    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
   830→    local extracted
   831→    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")
   832→
   833→    # If extraction changed the output, use it
   834→    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
   835→        echo "$extracted"
   836→        return 0
   837→    fi
   838→
   839→    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
   840→    # Match from first { to last }
   841→    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
   842→        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
   843→    else
   844→        echo "$output"
   845→    fi
   846→}
   847→
   848→# ============================================================================
   849→

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - GROQ API
#   - OpenRouter API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - groq: GROQ API via curl
#   - openrouter: OpenRouter API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Internal: Log LLM invocation completion with timing and cost metrics
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, openai-api, custom)
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - wall time in milliseconds
# Reads globals set by provider implementations:
#   _LLM_DURATION_MS - API-reported duration (may differ from wall time)
#   _LLM_COST_USD - Cost in USD or "unknown"
#   _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens or "unknown"
#   _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens or "unknown"
_llm_log_completion() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local wall_time_ms="$3"

    # Build timing component (show api_time only if different from wall_time)
    local timing="wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms"
    if [ -n "${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-}" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "unknown" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "$wall_time_ms" ]; then
        timing="${timing}, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS}ms"
    fi

    # Build token component
    local tokens="unknown"
    if [ "${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ] || [ "${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ]; then
        tokens="${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"
    fi

    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=$provider, model=$model, $timing, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=$tokens"
}

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        groq)
            # GROQ uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for groq provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        openrouter)
            # OpenRouter uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openrouter provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI (text content only)
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds
#     _LLM_COST_USD - Total cost in USD (if available)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --output-format json \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract metadata from JSON response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and cost information
    _llm_log_completion "claude-cli" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract just the result text from JSON
    local result
    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')

    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract result from Claude CLI JSON response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$result"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenAI API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg

# Internal: Invoke GROQ API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from GROQ API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (GROQ doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_groq() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${GROQ_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "GROQ API key not found. Set LLM_GROQ_API_KEY in config or GROQ_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking GROQ API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "GROQ API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "GROQ API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # GROQ doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "groq" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from GROQ response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    # Set metadata (unknown for custom provider since format is not standardized)
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS="unknown"

    # Log timing information
    _llm_log_completion "custom" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get provider from config
    local provider
    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        groq)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_groq "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openrouter)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openrouter "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, groq, openrouter, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}
